title: Fix: pudl import fails with 'Failed to initialize enhanced importer'
description:
Running 'pudl import --path test/data/iam_roles.json' produces:

Error: Failed to initialize enhanced importer

Suggestions:
  • Check system resources and permissions
  • Verify disk space is available
  • Ensure required dependencies are installed

Find the root cause and fix it. Start by tracing where this error message originates in the code, check what initialization steps the enhanced importer performs, and determine what's failing. Make sure all tests pass after the fix.

title: Implementation plan: Collection wrapper detection + unwrap at import time (Option A)
description:
Create a detailed implementation plan for Option A from pudl-yqt research.

Reference docs:
- .ai/research/pudl-yqt-collection-wrapper-schema.md (Doodle's research, Option A details)
- .ai/research/pudl-mxk-option-b-cue-schema-wrapper-detection.md (confirms CUE can't do this, Option A is the way)

Option A summary: Add wrapper detection in importer.go between data analysis and schema inference. When detected, extract the array of items, import them as a collection (reusing createCollectionItems), store wrapper metadata, and pass CollectionType hint for item schema inference.

The plan should cover:
1. internal/importer/wrapper.go — DetectCollectionWrapper with scoring heuristics
2. Integration into import path in importer.go
3. Fix CollectionType hint propagation
4. Known key name lists (wrapper keys, attribute keys, pagination keys)
5. Test strategy — positive cases (various API patterns) and negative cases (resources with array attrs)
6. Edge cases and error handling

Output: A concrete implementation plan with clear task breakdown, file-by-file changes, and dependency order. Write the plan to docs/plan.md (or update if exists).

title: Research: Robust implementation of CUE schema-based collection wrapper detection (Option B)
description:
Follow-up to pudl-yqt research. Doodle's doc recommended Option A (import-time unwrap) but we want to explore how Option B (built-in CUE schema) could be implemented robustly.

Option B as described: Define a new CUE schema #CollectionWrapper that structurally matches wrapper patterns using CUE constraints — a fallback schema matching objects with a single dominant array-of-objects field.

The noted challenges with Option B were:
- CUE can't easily express 'has exactly one field matching pattern X' without knowing the key name
- Would need to be very permissive (high false positive risk) or require specific key names (missing custom names)

Research tasks:
1. Deep dive into CUE's constraint system — what structural patterns CAN it express? Can we use open structs, disjunctions, or custom validators to match 'object with one dominant array-of-objects field'?
2. Explore whether CUE definitions could be combined with pudl's existing TypeExtractor/TypePattern to provide the heuristic layer that pure CUE can't handle
3. Investigate whether a family of CUE schemas (one per known wrapper key name) could work vs a single generic schema
4. Consider how the scoring heuristics from pudl-yqt (pagination siblings, count matching, negative signals) could integrate with schema-based detection
5. Assess false positive risk compared to Option A and propose mitigation strategies
6. Make a concrete recommendation for robust Option B implementation, or explain why Option A is strictly better

Reference: .ai/research/pudl-yqt-collection-wrapper-schema.md

Document findings in .ai/research/ per research rules.
close_reason: Research complete. CUE cannot express the structural constraint needed for generic wrapper detection. Option A (import-time unwrap) is recommended. Full findings in .ai/research/pudl-mxk-option-b-cue-schema-wrapper-detection.md

title: Research: Built-in schema for API collection wrapper responses
description:
Many APIs return collections of objects as objects with a key containing a list of items (e.g. {"Items": [...], "Count": 42}). The wrapper key is usually but not always called "Items" or similar. There may be other keys as well, but the response is effectively an object wrapping a collection.

We need a built-in core fallback schema (or catch-all) for matching these sorts of responses. Pudl treats collections differently from individual items, so we want to recognize these responses as collections when that's what they represent. However, we don't want to accidentally classify a single resource that just happens to have a key which is an array of objects as a collection.

Research tasks:
1. Review current pudl collection detection logic and schema matching
2. Survey common API collection wrapper patterns (Items, data, results, records, entries, etc.)
3. Identify heuristics for distinguishing true collection wrappers from single resources with array fields
4. Consider options for enhancing the built-in schema (e.g. key name matching, structure analysis, pagination signal detection)
5. Make a recommendation for the best approach

Document findings in .ai/research/ per research rules.
close_reason: Research complete. Findings documented in .ai/research/pudl-yqt-collection-wrapper-schema.md. Recommends Option A: wrapper detection + unwrap at import time.
